extraction.one_line_summary: BILBAO Payo won from a large breakaway, attacking in final kilometers and outsprinting ZIMMERMAN and O'CONNOR to move into 5th overall.
tactical_patterns: Peloton allowed 3-minute gap to large breakaway containing Bilbao (11th GC), ceding major GC time without chase response.
O'Connor repeatedly attacked final kilometers showing stage-18-Alps form, forcing Bilbao to use Zimmerman's pace rather than solo.
Sepp proved strongest Vingegaard domestique across multiple Tour stages, only teammate consistently surviving to key moments.

rider_signals.BILBAO Payo: positive: moved from 11th to 5th GC via breakaway stage win, showing aggressive intent for 3-minute gains versus top GC
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: positive: returned to 2021 Tour form with repeated late attacks, nearly caught winning move after bridging
rider_signals.SEPP Michael: positive: confirmed as Vingegaard's most reliable domestique, last man standing every stage except Puy de Dome
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Remy: negative: attacked immediately after being caught, wasted energy without reading peloton's willingness to let breakaway go
